content.boundary: Applies to state-sector (Klebelsberg Centre-governed) 6/8-year gimnázium capacity allocation; does not extend authority to church, foundation, or private maintainers, and does not alter admission-mechanism design within remaining places
content.change_lever: Formalize and extend the existing 2024/25 recommended 70-point entrance thresholds and capacity ceilings as binding national policy, applied specifically to state-maintained 6/8-year programmes, deliberately reducing selective-track share of the cohort over a multi-year horizon
content.name: Score-threshold and capacity-cap contraction of the selective state-sector track
content.system_problem: Stagnant physical capacity (~25,962 students) amid extreme oversubscription (9-26x) combined with policy-driven redirection of students toward vocational training indicates active supply-side contraction is already underway informally; a coherent, transparent contraction policy is absent
